title: Old emails displayed as new in unified inbox.
creator: qwantusx
content: Good day everyone. Have been an ignorant gmail user for years. Now, I am testing new grounds, and decided to move to more privacy focused email client. Only been using thunderbird for few weeks, but now it seems that my inbox is getting more confusing/confused that you would normally expect. Have not looked in too deep, but it seems that 10 year old emails appear as new in inbox. Say newest email is received on Sunday, and it says Sunday, but I was not expecting any email on Sunday, from that particular sender. So I looked ant the date and time, expecting it to be a spam, but this particular emais seems legit, however, the received date is Monday, 8 June, 2015. And that's not the first eMail from the past. I have got few more down in the inbox, like last week's, but they actually are from 2015. How is it even possible? I also have few new emails, so it's a real mishmash of new and old in the past few days. The only thing I can think of, is that I have changed the sync messages from (if I remember correctly) 6 months to anytime recently. Any ideas or hints?

content:
I've been "sending" e-mail from my Android phone for six weeks now. It looks like it's going out, but it never gets outside the domain.

I found the suggestion that I check my SMTP settings, but I used a wizard within TB for Mac so I cannot find the SMTP settings on the phone.

The wizard wasn't very wizard: I don't have the contacts stored within TB or the mail from my computer or anything. All I can do is receive e-mail.

I really need to get this working. My computer has died, and everything is going via the phone, and I only use e-mail for communication, not voice or text. (Long story, please don't argue with me about that.)

I just updated Android again yesterday, and TB should be upgrading automatically, if I have this thing set right.

Update: I have found the SMTP settings. They look correct.

My sister's e-mail is working, so the problem is definitely with my settings somehow.

Does anyone have any ideas? I really want to stick with TB.

Update 2: I found the debugging settings and turned on extra logging, but I can't find the logs themselves. Could someone point me to them?

I also tried the TB beta because it says explicitly that it does POP3, but that didn't seem to help either.

Now that I can edit my question, how do I delete the answers that are actually just updates?

Update 4: A different app works. I'd rather use TB if it can be arranged, though. . . .

Any help would be appreciated!

Thank you!

title: Thunderbird for Mobile
creator: egtaylor1
content:
Hello guys,

I've been a passionate user of (and advocate for) Thunderbird for more than 20 years now and was quite delighted when Thunderbird for Mobile was launched. At last I could use Thunderbird on my phone and tablet as well as my desktop! I've used Bluemail (the best of the rest in my view) on my mobile for some time and am currently comparing its performance side by side with Thunderbird for Mobile, having just installed the latter. In terms of fetching message I'm actually finding Bluemail much speedier at the moment and don't know why this is so. For example, I can often read incoming emails on Bluemail five minutes or more before they arrive via Thunderbird. So far as I know, I think I've tweaked all the necessary settings on Thunderbird but may be overlooking something. Can anybody suggest a way to speed up Thunderbird? I'm more than keen to keep on using it.

Cheers,

Grant

title: Android版ThunderbirdでOutlook、hotmailが追加できない
creator: kryyyy
content:
GooglePlayのレビュー5/27のものを参考に、Outlook側でIMAPの許可設定を行なってもエラーが発生してアカウントの追加ができません。

Windows版でもOutlookの追加にはエラーが出ましたが、こちらは解決しています。このエラーは調べたところ解決策が出てきたので、「アカウントハブ（実験的）」をオフにし、送信サーバーの設定を手入力したところ解決しました。

しかし、Android側では手入力で送信サーバーを入力する欄も出ず、認証後ひたすらエラーが出るばかりです。※Windows版で出た挙動と同じ

Android側への移行はQRでのインポートを行い、Gmail、独自ドメインは問題なく追加できています。

追加出来る方法があればご教授お願いいたします。

また、他の事象でエラーや困った時の解決参考になるものが、GooglePlayの回答にはあるものの、フォーラムでは何も出てこないため、近い事象はサポートで検索出来る状態だとありがたいです。

title: Awful changes in version 11.1 - Compared to 10.1
creator: whitedavidp
content:
I have been using TB Android for some time now. I saw the update to 11.1 from my current 10.1 this morning. So I jumped on it with excitement! Suffice it to say that, thanks to my rooted phone and Neo Backup, I have returned to 10.1...

My main problems are with the "hierarchical folders" - especially in gmail, which is the only place I see these bad changes (I have outlook and yahoo accounts as well):

1. There should be an option to turn use of "hierarchical folders" OFF in an account. I sure don't see that. 2. If "hierarchical folders" are to be insisted-upon, the state of opened folders in the hierarchy should be persisted - at the very least within a given TB session - but it would be more ideal across TB sessions. 3. If you tap on the un-opened "hierarchy" folder, you get a completely worthless page (see attached). WTH? 4. There should be a way to indicate that a given "hierarchy" folder should NOT be hidden in the drop-down but should be placed at the top, outside the hierarchy. Would would have hoped that the "Show At Top" setting in the Manage Folders would do this, but it does NOT.

I didn't go much beyond this. I realized right then that this was not the update for me. So I tried to use the back button to return from TB to the launcher's home page (so I could go to Neo Backup and recover). Guess what? The back button no longer exits from TB to the launcher in 11.1 as it did in 10.1. I sure did not see this in the release notes.

This is a shame really.
metadata: ;useragent:Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:135.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/135.0;os:Android 12

title: Sending Email.
creator: kathi15
content:
Hi,

It seems that the last upgrade has broken something with sending emails. With my desktop version, I have no trouble sending and receiving emails. I am using version 142.0 (64-bit) of Thunderbird, and the desktop computer is Windows 10.

I have 2 issues sending emails.

First issue, my Pixel 9 Pro XL does not send emails anymore. It was working fine until recently. The email is stalled in the outbox. I tried reloading the outgoing server settings, I get an error message; see attached error message. My Android receives emails fine, but will not send them. My phone syncs well with my desktop. However, the outbox doesn't sync, so I can't send the stalled emails from the desktop when I'm back in my office.

Second issue is my POS program (Windward Software System 5) stopped sending out emails as well. I worked with the tech department at the software company, and they said it isn't an issue with them, nothing has changed with them, no upgrades or changes on their end. Some of our older machines here, that haven't received the recent upgrade with Thunderbird, are still sending emails fine. Just a couple of computers are having this trouble of not sending emails from out POS software, it's the ones that have received the Thunderbird upgrade. For the outgoing settings, we are using the default port 465, and the connection security is SSL/TLS. Authentication method: is a normal password. I am hoping you have an answer for me. Please don't share the attachment with the community forums. Everything else will be fine.

Thank you,

Kathi McNally
